title: echeveria × semiteres
intro: Echeveria × semiteres, also known as the 'Dudley' or 'Chocolate Ruffles' succulent, is a hybrid plant species within the Echeveria genus, characterized by its unique, ruffled, and compact rosettes. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Echeveria × semiteres has been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, Echeveria × semiteres is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it is valued for its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
